Question: Find the directional derivative $$D_{\mathbf{u}} f ( x , y )$$ of $$f ( x , y ) = 3 x^{2} y - 4 x y^{3} + 3 y^{2} - 4 x$$ in the direction of $$\mathbf{u} = ( \text{cos} \frac{\pi}{3} ) \mathbf{i} + ( \text{sin} \frac{\pi}{3} ) \mathbf{j}$$ using Equation 4.37 . What is $$D_{\mathbf{u}} f ( 3 , 4 ) ?$$
Find the gradient $$\nabla f ( x , y )$$ of $$f ( x , y ) = ( x^{2} - 3 y^{2} ) \text{/} ( 2 x + y ) .$$
Find the direction for which the directional derivative of $$g ( x , y ) = 4 x - x y + 2 y^{2}$$ at $$( -2 , 3 )$$ is a maximum. What is the maximum value?
Answer:
The gradient of $$g$$ at $$( -2 , 3 )$$ is $$
abla g ( -2 , 3 ) = \mathbf{i} + 14 \mathbf{j} .$$ The unit vector that points in the same direction as $$
abla g ( -2 , 3 )$$ is $$ rac{
abla g ( -2 , 3 )}{\Vert 
abla g ( -2 , 3 ) \Vert} =  rac{1}{\sqrt{197}} \mathbf{i} +  rac{14}{\sqrt{197}} \mathbf{j} =  rac{\sqrt{197}}{197} \mathbf{i} +  rac{14 \sqrt{197}}{197} \mathbf{j} ,$$ which gives an angle of $$	heta = 	ext{arcsin} ( ( 14 \sqrt{197} ) 	ext{/} 197 ) \approx 1.499 	ext{rad} .$$ The maximum value of the directional derivative is $$\Vert 
abla g ( -2 , 3 ) \Vert = \sqrt{197} .$$

Question: For the function $$f ( x , y ) = x^{2} - 2 x y + 5 y^{2} + 3 x - 2 y + 4 ,$$ find the tangent to the level curve at point $$( 1 , 1 ) .$$ Draw the graph of the level curve corresponding to $$f ( x , y ) = 9$$ and draw $$\nabla f ( 1 , 1 )$$ and a tangent vector.
Answer: $$\nabla f ( x , y ) = ( 2 x - 2 y + 3 ) \mathbf{i} + ( -2 x + 10 y - 2 ) \mathbf{j}$$ $$\nabla f ( 1 , 1 ) = 3 \mathbf{i} + 6 \mathbf{j}$$ Tangent vector: $$6 \mathbf{i} - 3 \mathbf{j}$$ or $$-6 \mathbf{i} + 3 \mathbf{j}$$

Question: Find the gradient $$\nabla f ( x , y , z )$$ of $$f ( x , y , z ) = \frac{x^{2} - 3 y^{2} + z^{2}}{2 x + y - 4 z} .$$
Answer: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}\nabla f ( x , y , z ) & = \frac{2 x^{2} + 2 x y + 6 y^{2} - 8 x z - 2 z^{2}}{( 2 x + y - 4 z )^{2}} \mathbf{i} - \frac{x^{2} + 12 x y + 3 y^{2} - 24 y z + z^{2}}{( 2 x + y - 4 z )^{2}} \mathbf{j} \\  & + \frac{4 x^{2} - 12 y^{2} - 4 z^{2} + 4 x z + 2 y z}{( 2 x + y - 4 z )^{2}} \mathbf{k} .\end{matrix}\right .$$

Question: Calculate $$D_{\mathbf{u}} f ( x , y , z )$$ and $$D_{\mathbf{u}} f ( 0 , -2 , 5 )$$ in the direction of $$\text{v} = -3 \mathbf{i} + 12 \mathbf{j} - 4 \mathbf{k}$$ for the function $$f ( x , y , z ) = 3 x^{2} + x y - 2 y^{2} + 4 y z - z^{2} + 2 x z .$$
Answer: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aaa}D_{\mathbf{u}} f ( x , y , z ) & = & - \frac{3}{13} ( 6 x + y + 2 z ) + \frac{12}{13} ( x - 4 y + 4 z ) - \frac{4}{13} ( 2 x + 4 y - 2 z ) \\ D_{\mathbf{u}} f ( 0 , -2 , 5 ) & = & \frac{384}{13}\end{matrix}\right .$$
